pr.title: Match 11 functions across stage and fighter TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: Multi-TU matching PR touching fighter, stage, and interface code. It adds or improves implementations in ftcpuattack, ftCo_Attack100, ifstatus, gricemt, grpura, and grfourside, plus header signature refinements for ftcpuattack and gricemt. The bot report confirms 11 new matched items, but one listed item is .sdata and grPura_80212024 is reported as an unmatched improvement, so the PR body's '11 matched functions' claim has mixed support in the loaded evidence.

key_files.role: Adds ftCo_800D72A0 and ftCo_800D74A4 as score improvements and implements fn_800DB6C8 as a reported 100% match; uses victim/captor fighter fields, kind-specific item cleanup, PAD_STACK, and a scoped dont_inline pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_Attack100.c additions for ftCo_800D72A0, ftCo_800D74A4, fn_800DB6C8
text_corpus issue_comment: fn_800DB6C8 0.00% -> 100.00%; ftCo_800D74A4 and ftCo_800D72A0 listed as unmatched improvements

key_files.role: Implements ftCo_800B6208 as weighted random selection over a local ftCo_AttackEntry structure, including null-terminated scan, near-zero sum guard, and assert fallback.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct ftCo_AttackEntry and int ftCo_800B6208(ftCo_AttackEntry* arr)

key_files.role: Refines ftCo_800B6208 prototype from UNK_T to struct ftCo_AttackEntry* and adds forward declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800B6208 prototype changed to int ftCo_800B6208(struct ftCo_AttackEntry*)
human_pr_text: header refinement required for typed signature in matched body

key_files.role: Implements ifStatus_802F7034, reading gm_8016AE38 status data, converting zero/nonzero indices, and dispatching to ifStatus_802F6EA4 based on unk_B, match_result, and x24C8.x5_1.

Refines Ice Mountain stage code: static Vec3 lookup table, joint translation setup, wave/cloud update call shape, material refresh loops, dirty-bit joint rebinding, and grIceMt_801F9ACC signature body alignment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIm_803B8220 made static; grIceMt_801F72D4, grIceMt_801F7728, grIceMt_801F7F1C, grIceMt_801F81B4, grIceMt_801F98A8, grIceMt_801F9ACC changes
text_corpus issue_comment: grIceMt_801F7728, grIceMt_801F7F1C, grIceMt_801F81B4, grIceMt_801F98A8, grIceMt_801F72D4 listed as 100% matched items

key_files.role: Updates grIceMt_801F98A8 return type to void and expands grIceMt_801F9ACC prototype to include HSD_GObjEvent and Ground_GObj* arguments.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grIceMt_801F98A8 bool -> void; grIceMt_801F9ACC two args -> four args
human_pr_text: header refinements required by matched bodies and call site

key_files.role: Improves Pokemon Stadium stage routines including grPura_80212024, grPura_80212290, grPura_802125F0 field use, and grPura_80213030 pointer stepping. Bot confirms grPura_80212290 as 100%; grPura_80212024 remains an improvement in bot evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grPura_80212024, grPura_80212290, grPura_802125F0, grPura_80213030 changes

key_files.role: Corrects mpJointSetCb1 registration argument in grFourside_801F2F34 from Ground* to Ground_GObj*.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpJointSetCb1(0, gp, ...) changed to mpJointSetCb1(0, gobj, ...)

what_changed: Implemented ftCo_800B6208 as weighted random command selection from a null-terminated ftCo_AttackEntry array with HSD_Randf sampling and zero/near-zero fallback to 0.
Refined ftcpuattack.h so ftCo_800B6208 takes struct ftCo_AttackEntry* instead of UNK_T.

Added ftCo_Attack100 implementations/improvements: ftCo_800D72A0 checks current motion ranges from fp->x2D0, ftCo_800D74A4 computes velocity and motion id for a common attack path, and fn_800DB6C8 performs capture-pulled cleanup with kind-specific attached item cleanup.
Implemented ifStatus_802F7034 dispatch over gm_8016AE38 status fields, including -1 sentinel conversion for zero-valued index fields.

In gricemt.c, made grIm_803B8220 static and refined several Ice Mountain routines: joint translation setup, wave/cloud update, two material-refresh helper loops, dirty-bit joint rebind callback, and grIceMt_801F9ACC call/signature shape.
In gricemt.h, changed grIceMt_801F98A8 to void and changed grIceMt_801F9ACC to accept HSD_GObjEvent and Ground_GObj* in addition to gobj and float.
In grpura.c, refined Pokemon Stadium initializer/model setup and nearby field/pointer use, including render callback assignment and toon texture setup in grPura_80212290.
In grfourside.c, changed grFourside_801F2F34 to pass the ground GObj to mpJointSetCb1 rather than the Ground user-data pointer.
smart_moves: Header prototypes were updated alongside the recovered bodies instead of leaving UNK_T or speculative return types in public declarations.
The Ice Mountain material-refresh helpers were expressed as short two-iteration loops over gp->gv.icemt.xF8[] rather than preserving generated-looking repeated code.
The Fourside one-line fix targeted the callback registration argument directly, minimizing unrelated churn.

The PR body included narrow verification claims: full ninja SHA1 match, per-function objdiff-cli 100% reports, and no adjacent fuzzy regressions; bot output independently reports matched-code increase and per-item match status.
The ftcpuattack function introduced a local structure for cmd and weight fields, improving reviewability over an untyped UNK_T parameter and raw offsets.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered function body proves an argument type or return type, update the owning header in the same change rather than leaving UNK_T or a speculative bool signature.
scope: matched function bodies with call-site evidence
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800B6208 prototype changed from UNK_T to struct ftCo_AttackEntry*
raw_diff: grIceMt_801F9ACC prototype expanded to four parameters
human_pr_text: header refinements required by typed signature and call-site type checking

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fixed-count repeated helper behavior can match cleanly as a natural loop when the repeated operation is indexed over a cached array.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: both helpers use for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) over gp->gv.icemt.xF8[i]
text_corpus issue_comment: both helpers improved to 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Callback registration mismatches may be solved by checking whether an API expects the GObj wrapper rather than the user-data struct.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: mpJointSetCb1 argument changed from gp to gobj

decomp_lessons.lesson: A null-terminated weighted table can be recovered as a typed local struct with explicit cmd and weight fields, preserving the sentinel scan and degenerate-weight fallback.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_AttackEntry cmd at +00 and weight at +18
raw_diff: ftCo_800B6208 scans until p->cmd == 0 and returns 0 on empty or near-zero sum

naming_conventions.name: ftCo_AttackEntry
naming_conventions.kind: local typedef and header forward declaration
naming_conventions.evidence: The struct name is introduced for ftCo_800B6208 entries with cmd and weight fields and is forward-declared in ftcpuattack.h.

naming_conventions.name: victim_fp
naming_conventions.evidence: fn_800DB6C8 names the captured victim fighter pointer victim_fp, matching its use for victim kind and item cleanup.

naming_conventions.name: gobj
naming_conventions.evidence: Several functions use gobj instead of arg0 where the value is treated as an HSD/Ground object wrapper, including grIceMt_801F7728 and material helpers.

naming_conventions.name: a, b, c, unkB, mr, big_thing
naming_conventions.evidence: ifStatus_802F7034 still uses low-semantic or placeholder names for incompletely understood status fields.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK used narrowly to preserve stack shape in several functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Loaded PR evidence contains verification claims and bot scores, but does not explain why each PAD_STACK was required.

evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on before fn_800DB6C8 and #pragma pop after it
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The scope is tight, but the loaded evidence does not include a specific objdiff excerpt showing that dont_inline was unavoidable.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Natural for loops over fixed two-element material arrays replaced generated-looking do/while placeholders in grIceMt_801F7F1C and grIceMt_801F81B4.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) over gp->gv.icemt.xF8[i]
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This aligns with the loaded natural-loops standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Bitfield-style access replaced raw byte mask manipulation for grIceMt_801F98A8 dirty flag.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: flags byte and mask removed; ((UnkFlagStruct*) &gp->gv.icemt2.xC4)->b1 read and cleared
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports the changed access pattern in this PR, but UnkFlagStruct remains a generic name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use of direct field/union access and M2C_FIELD remains mixed: ftcpuattack gains a typed struct, while grPura_80213030 still uses M2C_FIELD for unresolved layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_AttackEntry typed fields cmd and weight
raw_diff: grPura_80213030 still uses M2C_FIELD(var_r31, HSD_JObj**, 8) and M2C_FIELD(var_r31, s16*, 0)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR improves some typing but does not fully resolve all nearby layouts.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.summary: Bot report for GALE01 shows matched code +0.06% (+2388 bytes), matched data +24 bytes, 11 new matched items, and 6 improvements in unmatched items.
No review comments or reviews are present in the loaded metadata.

searchable_terms: Match 11 functions across stage and fighter TUs
follow_up_queries: ftCo_800B6208 ftCo_AttackEntry struct ownership header placement
grIceMt_801F98A8 UnkFlagStruct b1 xC4 dirty bit

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR summary and verification claims
text_corpus issue_comment: decomp-dev bot report with per-item scores
notes: Strong evidence exists for changed files, concrete code edits, and bot-reported per-item match/improvement status.

The PR body claims 11 matched functions and per-function 100% objdiff for each of 11 functions, but the bot report lists 11 new matched items including .sdata and lists grPura_80212024 only as an unmatched improvement to 93.58%. This conflict should be preserved for curator review.
No human review comments are loaded.
Verification commands are PR-author claims in the body plus bot report; no raw objdiff logs are loaded.
PAD_STACK and dont_inline usage is visible, but exact necessity is not explained beyond match-score context.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header signatures should follow recovered matched bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2461 shows body-driven header cleanup: ftCo_800B6208 changes from UNK_T to struct ftCo_AttackEntry*, grIceMt_801F98A8 changes from speculative bool to void, and grIceMt_801F9ACC gains the event and Ground_GObj arguments needed by its recovered call site.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer natural fixed-count loops for repeated cached-material refresh helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grIceMt_801F7F1C and grIceMt_801F81B4, two repeated material refresh operations are recovered as for-loops over gp->gv.icemt.xF8[i], and the bot report shows both helpers reached 100%.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Callback APIs may require object wrappers, not user-data structs
accepted_candidate_records.text: grFourside_801F2F34 reached 100% after changing mpJointSetCb1's second argument from gp to gobj, indicating the callback registration expected the Ground_GObj wrapper at that call site.

source_update_candidates.title: ftcpuattack.h owns ftCo_800B6208's typed prototype
source_update_candidates.text: PR2461 changed ftCo_800B6208 from int ftCo_800B6208(UNK_T) to int ftCo_800B6208(struct ftCo_AttackEntry*) and added a forward declaration for struct ftCo_AttackEntry.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftcpuattack.c typedef and function body
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about prototype ownership and recovered argument type; curator should verify current source before accepting.

source_update_candidates.title: gricemt.h owns grIceMt_801F98A8 and grIceMt_801F9ACC signature refinements
source_update_candidates.text: PR2461 changed grIceMt_801F98A8 to return void and changed grIceMt_801F9ACC to take HSD_GObj*, float, HSD_GObjEvent, and Ground_GObj*.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about recovered stage function signatures; curator should verify against current source.

source_update_candidates.title: Require explanation for PAD_STACK and dont_inline in intake records
source_update_candidates.text: PR2461 uses several PAD_STACK calls and a tightly scoped #pragma dont_inline around fn_800DB6C8. The loaded evidence shows match-score context but not the specific codegen necessity for each tactic.
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces the existing standard that matching tactics need explicit evidence; curator may decide whether a source note or checklist update is useful.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's '11 matched functions' phrasing as an unqualified fact: loaded bot evidence lists 11 new matched items but includes .sdata and lists grPura_80212024 as only 93.58%.
Do not treat all PAD_STACK or dont_inline uses here as generally acceptable style; the loaded evidence lacks per-tactic necessity details.
Do not infer final semantics for placeholder names such as big_thing, unkB, a, b, c, or UnkFlagStruct beyond what the diff shows.
Do not promote grPura_80212024 as fully matched from this slice; the bot report contradicts that specific claim.
